title: AI Liquid Cooling Step-Function: Vera Rubin Forces 120-190 kW Racks
summary: Nvidia's Vera Rubin ramp turns liquid cooling from an option into a requirement, and the only two public-equity pure-plays — Vertiv and Modine — are printing sold-out backlogs that the sell-side multiple still doesn't reflect.
catalyst: On May 20, 2026 Nvidia confirmed Vera Rubin samples are already in customer hands with the GB300/Rubin NVL72/NVL144 ramp beginning H2 2026 [1]. Today (May 26), Modine reported Q4 FY26 data-center sales up 158% YoY and FY27 EBITDA guidance of $650-680M, 38-44% growth [2]. One month earlier, Vertiv reported Q1 backlog over $15B (more than doubled YoY) with raised 2026 guidance to $13.5-14.0B [3] and acquired Strategic Thermal Labs on April 27 to deepen cold-plate IP [4].
thesis: Vera Rubin pushes per-rack power from ~80 kW (Blackwell) to 120-130 kW (NVL72) and 190 kW (NVL144), beyond what air cooling can handle [5]. That forces hyperscalers to retrofit thermal infrastructure at every greenfield AI factory — and Vertiv is a named primary partner on Nvidia's DSX reference design [6]. Modine is the only other US-listed pure-play scaling fast enough to win share, with data-center revenue on track from <$200M in FY24 to a $2B target by FY28 [2].
body_md:
## Setup

The data-center cooling business has spent twenty years as a 4-5% growth backwater. AI has broken that. Per-rack thermal density is rising faster than any time since the mainframe era, and at Vera Rubin's launch density it crosses a hard physical threshold: air can no longer remove the heat. Nvidia's reference design (DSX, released March 16, 2026) now assumes liquid cooling as the default for AI factories, not the exception [6]. The companies that own the thermal chain from CDU through cold-plate are mid-cycle in the largest order book of their existence.

## Why Now

Three catalysts converged in the last 30 days:

1. **Nvidia Q1 FY27 (May 20, 2026)**: $81.6B revenue, $39.1B data-center segment, +69% YoY. Jensen confirmed Vera Rubin samples are in customer hands and the ramp begins H2 2026. Hyperscaler 2026 AI capex revised up to $725B [1].
2. **Modine Q4 FY26 (today, May 26)**: data-center sales +158% YoY in the quarter; FY26 total $3.18B (+23%); FY27 guidance net sales +20-35% with adjusted EBITDA of $650-680M (+38-44%) [2]. Management reiterated >$1B data-center sales for FY26 and a $2B target by FY28.
3. **Vertiv Q1 2026 (April 22)**: $2.65B revenue (+30%), adjusted operating margin 20.8% (+430 bps), backlog above $15B representing 12-18 months of forward revenue, 2026 guidance raised to $13.5-14.0B (29-31% organic) [3]. Then on April 27 Vertiv acquired Strategic Thermal Labs — a cold-plate and server-side liquid-cooling specialist — to lock in chip-level thermal IP [4].

These are not narrative data points. They are sold-out order books at companies that, two years ago, were not considered AI plays.

## Why Vera Rubin Is the Step-Function

Morgan Stanley's BOM analysis of Nvidia's rack platforms puts per-rack liquid cooling content at roughly $49,860 for Blackwell Ultra NVL72 (18 compute trays at $2,260 each, 9 switch trays at $1,020 each) and $55,710 for Vera Rubin NVL144, a 17% step-up on higher-capacity cold plates and hotter NVSwitch 6.0 ASICs [5]. The full Rubin platform draws 120-130 kW per rack (NVL72) and 190 kW per rack (NVL144), with later "Rubin Ultra" configurations targeting up to 600 kW.

At 120+ kW, air cooling is not a degraded option — it is physically inadequate. Every Rubin deployment is a liquid-cooled deployment. That is a binary forcing function on the supply chain.

## Company-Specific Edge

### Vertiv (VRT) — the prime contractor

Vertiv is one of 13 primary partners named in Nvidia's Vera Rubin DSX AI Factory reference design announcement [6], alongside Schneider Electric, Eaton, Siemens, and Trane. Within that group it is the only US-listed name with a converged power-and-cooling product set built specifically for AI factories — "Vertiv OneCore Rubin DSX" — and the 800VDC MGX reference architecture that Nvidia adopted for next-generation racks.

The screener report scores VRT 82 (BUY), with Innovation 9/10 and AI Resilience 10/10. Returns on equity sit at 41.8% with adjusted operating margins now above 20%. EPS grew 199.5% year-over-year [screener]. The $15B+ backlog gives roughly 12-18 months of forward revenue visibility before any new Rubin orders flow through. At 31.5x forward earnings the multiple is full, but the EPS growth rate makes the PEG attractive relative to the breadth of the order book.

The Strategic Thermal Labs acquisition (April 27, 2026) is the tell. Vertiv already led in CDUs and rear-door heat exchangers; it acquired STL specifically for cold-plate design and server-side validation — the chip-interface layer that determines whether a 1.8 kW Rubin GPU can be cooled at all [4]. That is Vertiv moving up the value chain, not protecting share.

### Modine (MOD) — the asymmetric option

Modine is the more interesting setup. The legacy business is auto-parts thermal management (a low-margin, slow-growth segment management plans to spin off as "Performance Technologies" in FY27 [2]). The new business is data-center cooling, and it is exploding: data-center revenue grew 158% in Q4 FY26 and management reiterated growth of 50-70% annually for the next two years [2].

The screener report shows MOD with a PEG of 1.03, ROE of 10%, and revenue growth of 30.5% [screener]. AI Resilience is 9/10. The 33x forward P/E looks rich until you remember that mix shift toward Climate Solutions (which grew 40-45% in FY26 versus consolidated 23%) compresses the multiple naturally — a pure-play Climate Solutions Modine at $2B in FY28 data-center sales, at the segment's higher margins, is the asymmetric case.

The spin-off, once timing is set, will force the sum-of-the-parts disclosure that today's multiple does not require. That is a re-rating catalyst on its own.

## Numbers That Matter

| Metric | VRT | MOD |
|---|---|---|
| 2026 revenue guide / growth | $13.5-14.0B / +30% [3] | $3.18B FY26 / +23% [2] |
| Q1/Q4 data-center growth | Backlog >$15B (doubled) [3] | DC sales +158% YoY in Q4 [2] |
| FY27 outlook | (Implied 29-31% organic) [3] | EBITDA $650-680M, +38-44% [2] |
| Forward P/E (screener) | 31.5 | 33.2 |
| ROE / Margin (screener) | 41.8% / 13.0% | 10.0% / 3.4% |
| AI Resilience score (screener) | 10/10 | 9/10 |

## Bear Case

The multiples are not cheap. Vertiv at 31x forward and Modine at 33x leave little room for a hyperscaler capex pause. Three things would invalidate this trade:

1. **Hyperscaler capex cut.** If two of the four big hyperscalers (MSFT, META, GOOGL, AMZN) materially cut 2027 AI capex guidance, the order book unwinds quickly. Today the trajectory is the opposite — capex revised up to $725B — but it can turn.
2. **Direct-to-chip displacement.** If immersion cooling (Submer, GRC, LiquidStack) or microfluidic chip cooling moves from pilot to mainstream faster than expected, the cold-plate/CDU model that Vertiv and Modine sell becomes a stopgap. So far hyperscalers have rejected immersion at scale on serviceability grounds.
3. **Nvidia Rubin slip.** Any meaningful delay to Rubin's H2 2026 ramp pushes out the step-function. Nvidia's track record on schedule has been good, but not perfect (Blackwell slipped two quarters in 2025).

A fourth, smaller risk: Modine's spin-off execution. A messy separation could create a 12-18 month overhang even if the data-center business is firing.

## Conclusion

This is a pick-and-shovel trade on a forced architectural shift, not a bet on "more AI." Vera Rubin's thermal envelope removes the option of air cooling, Vertiv is named on the reference design, and Modine is the only other listed pure-play scaling fast enough to matter. Both companies are guiding to 23-31% revenue growth on order books they have already won. The market sees the AI tailwind; what it is still underwriting is the binary nature of the cooling shift inside Rubin racks.

Size Vertiv as the core position (visibility, margin, reference-design partnership), Modine as the asymmetric supplement (smaller, less liquid, spin-off optionality, mix shift compressing forward multiple).

ticker_rationale.VRT: Screener BUY at 82; $15B+ Q1 2026 backlog (more than doubled YoY) [3], 199.5% EPS YoY growth per the report, AI Resilience 10/10, ROE 41.8%. Named primary partner on Nvidia's Vera Rubin DSX reference design [6].
ticker_rationale.MOD: Screener HOLD at 77 with PEG 1.03; Q4 FY26 data-center sales +158% YoY, FY27 adjusted EBITDA guidance $650-680M (+38-44%) [2], $2B data-center FY28 target. Performance Technologies spin-off creates SOTP re-rating catalyst.

risks: Hyperscaler 2027 capex cut: if two of MSFT/META/GOOGL/AMZN guide 2027 AI capex below 2026 levels, both stocks unwind faster than fundamentals warrant given 31-33x forward multiples.
Direct chip cooling displacement: immersion or microfluidic cooling moving from pilot to scale earlier than 2028 reduces the addressable cold-plate market the STL acquisition is built around.
Vera Rubin schedule slip: any quarter-plus delay to the H2 2026 Rubin ramp pushes out the step-function inflection and gives skeptics ammunition on multiple.
Modine spin-off execution risk: messy separation of Performance Technologies creates a 12-18 month equity overhang independent of data-center fundamentals.
Competition from Schneider Electric (SU.PA): non-listed in the US, but the only peer with comparable Vera Rubin DSX partnership status and a deeper European installed base.
invalidation: Vertiv backlog declines quarter-over-quarter (below $15B) on a non-acquisition basis at any 2026 or 2027 earnings print.
Modine data-center segment year-over-year growth drops below 30% in any quarter through FY28, or the FY28 $2B target is withdrawn.
Nvidia announces Vera Rubin GA delay of two or more quarters past H2 2026.
Aggregate hyperscaler 2027 AI capex guidance issued at any point comes in below 2026 actuals (i.e., capex contraction year-over-year).
VRT or MOD adjusted operating margin contracts by more than 200 bps in any quarter without a clear one-time explanation, signaling pricing power erosion.
